references.text: [GuKa15] Guth, Larry and Katz, Nets Hawk, On the Erd\H{o}s distinct distances problem in the plane. Ann. of Math. (2) (2015), 155-190.

remarks.text: Perhaps the diameter is even $\geq n-1$ for sufficiently large $n$. Piepmeyer has an example of $9$ such points with diameter $<5$. Kanold proved the diameter is $\geq n^{3/4}$. The bounds on the distinct distance problem [89] proved by Guth and Katz \cite{GuKa15} imply a lower bound of $\gg n/\log n$.

statement.text: Let $A$ be a set of $n$ points in $\mathbb{R}^2$ such that all pairwise distances are at least $1$ and if two distinct distances differ then they differ by at least $1$. Is the diameter of $A$ $\gg n$?
